plan_analysis:
The question resolves to the percent-return spread ( CL minus ES ) during 16-27 Mar 2026, so forecasters mainly need two classes of information:  
a) Historical behaviour of that spread (base rate, distribution, seasonality, typical volatility) and its main drivers.  
b) Near-term catalysts between now and 27 Mar 2026 that could push either leg (oil or equities) sharply up or down.

The pre-research context only outlines what the indices represent; it contains no price history, no statistics on the CL–ES spread, and no mention of upcoming events affecting oil or equities. Gaps therefore include:  
• Time-series data for CL=F and ES=F closes to compute past bi-weekly spreads.  
• Long-run statistics and correlations of oil vs S&P 500 to anchor priors.  
• Identification of key drivers (OPEC+ policy, Middle-East/Russia supply risks, global demand data, Fed policy, U.S. macro releases) and the calendar of such events within 16-27 Mar 2026.  
• Very recent price moves and positioning that could signal momentum or reversals.  
• Contrarian information—e.g., reasons equities could outperform oil even if geopolitical risks are rising, or why oil could fall on surprise inventory builds.

Search strategy:  
1. Pull raw price histories for CL=F and ES=F (yFinance) for at least the last 10 years.  
2. Use Agent to synthesise the bi-weekly CL–ES spread statistics (mean, st-dev, extreme tails) to give a base-rate reference.  
3. Google for academic/industry studies on oil-equity return differentials and correlations to enrich structural understanding.  
4. Gather news on the most likely market movers in the 16-27 Mar window: OPEC+ meeting schedule, U.S. EIA inventory release calendar, Fed/FOMC, and any major geopolitical flashpoints.  
5. Scan current news flow for unusual inventory surprises, positioning data (CFTC COT), or macro-shocks already emerging.  
6. Include a contrarian-check query looking for bearish oil or bullish equity narratives despite prevailing sentiment.

queries_planned.intent: Download daily crude-oil futures close prices to calculate historical bi-weekly returns.
Download daily S&P 500 futures close prices to calculate historical bi-weekly returns.

queries_planned.query: CL minus ES biweekly returns distribution
queries_planned.intent: Find studies/data sets analysing the historical return spread between oil and S&P 500.
Obtain academic/industry papers giving long-run correlation and causation insights between crude oil and equity markets.

queries_planned.query: [HISTORICAL] Compute last-10-yrs biweekly percent-return spread between CL=F and ES=F, summarise mean, median, stdev, 10th/90th percentiles, and list top 10 positive and negative episodes. Also note any seasonal pattern around March.

queries_planned.intent: Get latest information on any OPEC/OPEC+ gathering that could affect oil prices inside the forecast window.

queries_planned.query: S&P 500 futures outlook ahead of March FOMC 2026
queries_planned.intent: Capture analyst expectations and potential equity-market drivers linked to the Fed decision occurring during the period.

queries_planned.query: crude oil inventory surprise March 2026 EIA
queries_planned.intent: Surface recent or anticipated U.S. EIA inventory reports and commentary that could swing oil prices, offering a contrarian check if inventories are building.
